cf_parsed.subtask_body:
Re-checked the current best against a few focused alternatives around the costly east/top-right section.
Current best tour edges are mostly short; the obvious pain points are 1-9 (~66), 9-17 (92), and 16-13 (~47.8), but changing one usually worsens another.
Tested east ordering ...18-11-17-1-9... versus ...18-11-1-9-17...:
The first saves 11-17 and 17-1, but then forces either 9 to connect back far west or creates 9-11/9-18 type jumps; rough total not better.
Tested moving 9 nearer top-center with patterns like ...3-9-1-11-17... and ...7-9-1-11-18...
These reduce 1-9 somewhat but add very large 3-9 or 7-9 plus bad exits from 17; net looked worse than current.
Checked swapping the tail to ...17-11-5... or ...17-1-11-5...; both introduce long 17-11 or 1-5/17-5 combinations.
Also checked whether replacing ...13-10-4-6-0... by ...13-4-10-6-0... or ...10-13-4... helps; all worse because 13-4 and 10-6 are both much longer.
A more perimeter-like full order such as 13-2-14-5-11-17-1-9-7-3-12-0-6-4-10-19-8-16-18-15 creates a very bad 9-7 or 15-13 closure and did not seem competitive.
Conclusion: the incumbent tour still looks like the strongest mentally verified option; I do not have a confidently better valid replacement.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Keep the incumbent valid tour. If another turn were allowed, only worthwhile work would be a narrow search over reconnecting city 9 with the east cluster and testing one or two 2-opt swaps involving edges (1,9), (9,17), and the closing edge (16,13).

parsed.subtask_body:
Built a perimeter-style noncrossing tour and compared rough edge sums against the baseline tour.
Candidate A: 13-10-4-6-0-12-3-7-15-18-11-1-9-17-5-14-2-19-8-16.
Approx edge lengths: 6.1,30.1,30.4,17.5,36.2,18.4,18.4,5.8,19.7,33.3,25.5,66.1,92.0,42.2,29.0,9.1,18.0,15.2,23.3,47.8 ≈ 584.0.
This already beats baseline 589.7 by ~5.7.
Tried patching the very bad 9-17 edge by rerouting east-side order, but alternatives tended to worsen more edges:
Using ...1-17-9... makes 1-17 + 9-next too large.
Using ...11-17-1-9... still leaves a huge 9-to-west return.
Tested replacing tail ...5-14-2-19-8-16-13 with ...5-2-14-19-8-16-13 and variants; these looked slightly worse.
The candidate is geometrically plausible: west chain up left side, across top center, down through right cluster, back along bottom and center-left.
Main remaining weakness is connecting top-right city 9 without paying one long edge; likely unavoidable at this size.
I do see another plausible candidate with 17 placed between 11 and 1 and 9 attached to 7/3 side, but quick estimates did not clearly beat 584.
So current best is Candidate A, valid and likely modestly better than baseline.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Keep the best valid tour found. One more subtask would focus only on the east-side/top-right placement of cities 1,9,11,17 and possible 2-opt swaps around 16/13 and 18/11, since that is where most excess length remains.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Start from geometric structure: split cities into left cluster, middle chain, and right/east cluster, aiming for a noncrossing perimeter-style cycle.
Use the baseline as a validity reference only; do not stay close to it if obvious crossings or long jumps appear.
First build a candidate by angular/perimeter intuition: bottom-left to bottom-right, up the east side, across top, then down the west side, inserting interior nodes near their natural neighbors.
Then compare against the baseline and patch the biggest likely excess edges, especially links to city 9 and between right-side cities 1/11/17 and center cities 7/15/18/16.
Check for crossing edges mentally and remove them via 2-opt style swaps.
If time remains, test a second ordering focused on the chain 2-13-10-4-6-0-12-3 and the chain 14-5-11-17-1-9, joined through middle cities.
Final turn should output the best full valid tour found, even if only modestly improved over baseline.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct and refine one strong noncrossing feasible tour using geometric clustering and mental 2-opt improvements", "p_solve": 0.55, "time_budget_s": 240}

This already beats baseline 589.7 by ~5.7.
Tried patching the very bad 9-17 edge by rerouting east-side order, but alternatives tended to worsen more edges:
Using ...1-17-9... makes 1-17 + 9-next too large.
Using ...11-17-1-9... still leaves a huge 9-to-west return.
Tested replacing tail ...5-14-2-19-8-16-13 with ...5-2-14-19-8-16-13 and variants; these looked slightly worse.
The candidate is geometrically plausible: west chain up left side, across top center, down through right cluster, back along bottom and center-left.
Main remaining weakness is connecting top-right city 9 without paying one long edge; likely unavoidable at this size.
I do see another plausible candidate with 17 placed between 11 and 1 and 9 attached to 7/3 side, but quick estimates did not clearly beat 584.
So current best is Candidate A, valid and likely modestly better than baseline.

UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Keep the best valid tour found. One more subtask would focus only on the east-side/top-right placement of cities 1,9,11,17 and possible 2-opt swaps around 16/13 and 18/11, since that is where most excess length remains.

CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.3,"expected_delta_score":1.2,"expected_gap_reduction":1.2}
DECISION: stop
